title: AISI says agent evaluations need to measure capability across compute budgets
friendlyTitle: AI agents may do better when they get more time
summary: The UK AI Security Institute argues that AI agent capability is not a single benchmark score but a curve over test-time compute. Its testing found that larger token budgets can expose substantially stronger results, especially in software engineering, cybersecurity, mathematics, and academic tasks.
friendlySummary: A UK safety lab says AI agent tests should show how performance changes when the agent gets more time and tokens to work.
whyItMatters: For businesses and users, an agent that fails quickly may not be incapable; it may simply have been given too little time or budget. Procurement, safety reviews, and product design will need to account for how much autonomy and compute an agent can spend.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This helps people judge AI tools more fairly. A tool that fails in a short test might still succeed if it is allowed to think, test, and try again.
